fact: Ralph Baer conceives the idea of playing games on a television set, beginning work on what would become the 'Brown Box' prototype.
The Magnavox Odyssey, the first commercial home video game console, is released in North America.
Atari is founded by Nolan Bushnell and Ted Dabney in Sunnyvale, California.
Pong, developed by Allan Alcorn at Atari, is installed as an arcade cabinet at Andy Capp's Tavern in Sunnyvale, CA.
Taito Corporation releases Space Invaders in Japan, designed by Tomohiro Nishikado. It causes a nationwide coin shortage.
The Atari 2600 (originally the Atari VCS) launches in North America.
Namco releases Pac-Man in arcades in Japan, designed by Toru Iwatani.
Pac-Man becomes the first video game character to be featured in a television series.
Shigeru Miyamoto's Donkey Kong debuts in arcades, introducing the character Jumpman (later Mario).
Microsoft Flight Simulator 1.0 is released for IBM PC, making it one of the longest-running game franchises.
The North American video game crash of 1983 begins, triggered by market saturation and poor-quality titles.
Atari secretly buries millions of unsold E.T. the Extra-Terrestrial cartridges in a New Mexico landfill.
Nintendo releases the Family Computer (Famicom) in Japan.
Dragon Quest, designed by Yuji Horii with art by Akira Toriyama, launches in Japan on the Famicom, creating the JRPG genre.
The Nintendo Entertainment System (NES) launches in North America with 18 launch titles.
Super Mario Bros. is released in Japan for the Famicom, designed by Shigeru Miyamoto and Takashi Tezuka.
The Legend of Zelda is released in Japan for the Famicom Disk System.
Sega releases the Master System in Japan (as the Sega Mark III).
Tetris is created by Alexey Pajitnov at the Soviet Academy of Sciences in Moscow.
Metroid is released in Japan for the Famicom Disk System, revealing Samus Aran as a female protagonist.
Konami's Castlevania is released in Japan for the Famicom Disk System.
Capcom releases the original Mega Man for the Famicom in Japan.
Square releases the original Final Fantasy in Japan for the Famicom, saving the company from bankruptcy.
Metal Gear, designed by Hideo Kojima, is released for the MSX2 in Japan.
The Sega Genesis (Mega Drive) launches in Japan.
Nintendo releases the Game Boy in Japan, bundled with Tetris.
Sega Genesis launches in North America, beginning the 16-bit console war against Nintendo.
SimCity is released for Macintosh, designed by Will Wright, pioneering the city-building genre.
Capcom releases Street Fighter II in arcades in Japan, igniting the fighting game boom.
Sonic the Hedgehog is released for the Sega Genesis in North America, establishing Sega's mascot.
The Super Nintendo Entertainment System (SNES) launches in North America.
The Super Famicom launches in Japan.
id Software releases Wolfenstein 3D, pioneering the first-person shooter genre.
Mortal Kombat debuts in arcades, sparking controversy over violent content.
Sega releases Sonic the Hedgehog 2, which debuts on 'Sonic 2sday' — a Tuesday global launch.
The Legend of Zelda: A Link to the Past is released in Japan for the Super Famicom.
Myst is released for Macintosh, becoming the best-selling PC game for nearly a decade.
id Software releases Doom, revolutionizing the FPS genre and popularizing online multiplayer deathmatch.
The Entertainment Software Rating Board (ESRB) is established in response to Congressional hearings on video game violence.
Sony releases the PlayStation in Japan, entering the console market for the first time.
The Sega Saturn launches in Japan.
Sega surprise-launches the Saturn in North America at E3, four months ahead of schedule, angering retailers.
Sony PlayStation launches in North America at $299, undercutting the Sega Saturn.
Chrono Trigger, developed by Square's 'Dream Team' (Hironobu Sakaguchi, Yuji Horii, Akira Toriyama), launches in Japan.
The Nintendo 64 launches in Japan with Super Mario 64, revolutionizing 3D platforming.
Quake by id Software is released, advancing 3D graphics and popularizing competitive online FPS gaming.
Pokémon Red and Green are released in Japan for the Game Boy, designed by Satoshi Tajiri.
Resident Evil (Biohazard) is released in Japan for the PlayStation, coining the term 'survival horror'.
Tomb Raider is released, introducing Lara Croft as one of gaming's most iconic characters.
Diablo is released by Blizzard North, defining the action RPG genre.
Nintendo 64 launches in North America.
Final Fantasy VII is released in Japan for the PlayStation, bringing JRPGs to mainstream Western audiences.
GoldenEye 007 is released for the Nintendo 64, proving FPS games could work on consoles.
Ultima Online launches, becoming one of the first graphical MMORPGs.
Gran Turismo is released in Japan for PlayStation, selling over 10 million copies worldwide.
StarCraft is released by Blizzard Entertainment, eventually becoming a national sport in South Korea.
The Legend of Zelda: Ocarina of Time launches for the Nintendo 64, widely considered one of the greatest games ever made.
Half-Life is released by Valve, revolutionizing storytelling in first-person shooters.
Metal Gear Solid is released in Japan for the PlayStation, directed by Hideo Kojima.
Sega releases the Dreamcast in Japan, the first console with a built-in modem for online play.
Sega Dreamcast launches in North America with record-breaking first-day sales of $98.4 million.
EverQuest launches, pioneering the modern MMORPG and earning the nickname 'EverCrack' for its addictive gameplay.
Super Smash Bros. is released in Japan for the Nintendo 64.
Counter-Strike is released as a Half-Life mod by Minh Le and Jess Cliffe, becoming the definitive competitive FPS.
Sony releases the PlayStation 2 in Japan, which would go on to become the best-selling console of all time (155+ million units).
The Sims is released by Maxis, designed by Will Wright, becoming one of the best-selling PC games ever.
Diablo II is released by Blizzard North.
PlayStation 2 launches in North America.
Sega announces it will discontinue the Dreamcast and exit the hardware business, becoming a third-party developer.
Nintendo releases the Game Boy Advance in Japan.
Microsoft enters the console market with the launch of the original Xbox in North America.
Halo: Combat Evolved launches alongside the Xbox, establishing the franchise as a console FPS icon.
Nintendo GameCube launches in Japan.
Grand Theft Auto III is released for PlayStation 2, popularizing the open-world sandbox genre.
Super Smash Bros. Melee is released for GameCube in Japan, later becoming a major competitive esports title.
Final Fantasy X is released in Japan, the first fully-voiced mainline Final Fantasy game.
Kingdom Hearts, a collaboration between Square and Disney, is released in Japan.
Xbox Live launches, bringing unified online console gaming to the masses.
Battlefield 1942 is released, pioneering large-scale online multiplayer warfare.
Metroid Prime is released for GameCube, successfully translating the 2D franchise into first-person 3D.
The Elder Scrolls III: Morrowind is released for PC and Xbox.
Warcraft III: Reign of Chaos is released by Blizzard, whose map editor would later spawn the MOBA genre via DotA.
Star Wars Galaxies launches as one of the first major licensed MMORPGs.
Valve releases Steam, initially as a platform for updating Counter-Strike.
Call of Duty is released by Infinity Ward, beginning one of gaming's most successful franchises.
Nintendo releases the Nintendo DS in North America, featuring dual screens and a touchscreen.
World of Warcraft launches in North America, eventually reaching 12 million subscribers.
Half-Life 2 is released by Valve, showcasing the Source engine and Havok physics.
Sony releases the PlayStation Portable (PSP) in Japan.
Halo 2 launches with $125 million in first-day sales, the largest entertainment launch at the time.
Grand Theft Auto: San Andreas is released for PS2, later sparking the 'Hot Coffee' controversy.
The 'Hot Coffee' mod controversy leads to GTA: San Andreas being temporarily re-rated to Adults Only by the ESRB.
Microsoft launches the Xbox 360, starting the seventh console generation.
Guitar Hero is released, igniting the rhythm game craze in the West.
Shadow of the Colossus is released in Japan for PlayStation 2, acclaimed as an artistic masterpiece.
Resident Evil 4 is released for GameCube, redefining the third-person shooter genre.
Brain Age: Train Your Brain in Minutes a Day! launches in Japan for the DS, popularizing 'brain training' games.
Nintendo launches the Wii in North America with Wii Sports, bringing motion controls to the mainstream.
Sony launches the PlayStation 3 in Japan.
PlayStation 3 launches in North America at $499/$599, drawing criticism for its high price.
The Elder Scrolls IV: Oblivion is released, becoming a landmark open-world RPG.
Wii Sports becomes the best-selling single-platform game ever at the time, bundled with the Wii.
Gears of War launches on Xbox 360, popularizing the cover-based shooter.
BioShock is released, acclaimed for its narrative depth and the iconic 'Would you kindly?' twist.
Portal is released by Valve as part of The Orange Box, introducing GLaDOS and 'the cake is a lie.'
Call of Duty 4: Modern Warfare is released, shifting the franchise from WWII to modern combat and revolutionizing online multiplayer.
Halo 3 earns $170 million on its first day, breaking entertainment launch records.
Rock Band is released, expanding the rhythm game genre to full band play.
Mass Effect is released by BioWare for Xbox 360, defining the cinematic RPG.
Super Mario Galaxy is released for Wii, receiving universal acclaim.
Grand Theft Auto IV earns $500 million in its first week, setting a new entertainment record.
LittleBigPlanet launches for PS3, pioneering user-generated content in console games.
Left 4 Dead is released by Valve, defining cooperative survival shooters.
Braid is released on Xbox Live Arcade, helping to launch the indie game renaissance.
Demon's Souls is released in Japan by FromSoftware, originating the 'Soulslike' genre.
Minecraft alpha is publicly released by Markus 'Notch' Persson.
League of Legends is released by Riot Games, becoming the world's most-played PC game.
Uncharted 2: Among Thieves launches for PS3, setting new standards for cinematic gaming.
Angry Birds is released for iOS by Rovio, becoming a mobile gaming phenomenon.
Wii Sports Resort launches, bundled with the Wii MotionPlus accessory for improved motion controls.
Bayonetta is released in Japan, directed by Hideki Kamiya at PlatinumGames.
Red Dead Redemption launches for PS3 and Xbox 360, acclaimed as one of the greatest open-world games.
StarCraft II: Wings of Liberty is released by Blizzard after 12 years of development.
Microsoft launches Kinect for Xbox 360, selling 8 million units in 60 days.
Super Meat Boy is released, becoming a flagship indie platformer title.
Heavy Rain launches for PS3, pushing interactive narrative games forward.
Minecraft officially releases (version 1.0) at MineCon in Las Vegas.
The Elder Scrolls V: Skyrim is released, selling 7 million copies in its first week.
Portal 2 is released by Valve, acclaimed for its writing and puzzle design.
Dark Souls is released in Japan by FromSoftware, popularizing punishing difficulty in action RPGs.
Nintendo launches the 3DS in Japan, featuring glasses-free 3D.
Sony launches the PlayStation Vita in Japan.
Twitch.tv launches as a spin-off from Justin.tv, dedicated to game streaming.
L.A. Noire is released, featuring groundbreaking MotionScan facial capture technology.
Nintendo Wii U launches in North America, the first eighth-generation console.
Journey is released for PS3 by thatgamecompany, redefining multiplayer and emotional gaming experiences.
Diablo III launches, with server issues causing the infamous 'Error 37' preventing millions from playing.
The Walking Dead by Telltale Games is released, reviving narrative adventure games.
DayZ mod for ARMA 2 is released, popularizing the survival game genre.
FTL: Faster Than Light launches via Kickstarter, proving crowdfunding as a viable game funding model.
Borderlands 2 is released, solidifying the looter-shooter genre.
Grand Theft Auto V is released, earning $800 million on day one and $1 billion within three days.
Sony launches the PlayStation 4 in North America.
Microsoft launches the Xbox One in North America, initially bundled with Kinect at $499.
The Last of Us is released for PS3 by Naughty Dog, acclaimed for its narrative.
Dota 2 officially launches by Valve after a lengthy beta, with The International prize pool exceeding $2.8 million.
BioShock Infinite is released, concluding the BioShock trilogy.
Flappy Bird, developed by Dong Nguyen in Vietnam, goes viral on mobile.
Flappy Bird is removed from app stores by its creator due to guilt over its addictive nature.
Facebook acquires Oculus VR for $2 billion, signaling major corporate interest in virtual reality.
Amazon acquires Twitch for $970 million.
Microsoft acquires Mojang (makers of Minecraft) for $2.5 billion.
Five Nights at Freddy's is released by Scott Cawthon, becoming an indie horror phenomenon.
Destiny launches by Bungie, with a reported $500 million budget making it one of the most expensive games ever.
Hearthstone: Heroes of Warcraft officially launches by Blizzard, popularizing digital card games.
The Gamergate controversy erupts, sparking major debates about harassment, ethics, and diversity in gaming.
P.T. (Playable Teaser) for Silent Hills is released on PS4, directed by Hideo Kojima and Guillermo del Toro.
Konami cancels Silent Hills and removes P.T. from the PlayStation Store, amid Kojima's departure.
The Witcher 3: Wild Hunt is released by CD Projekt Red, setting new standards for open-world RPGs.
Splatoon is released for Wii U, Nintendo's first major new IP in years.
Undertale is released by Toby Fox, becoming an indie RPG phenomenon.
Metal Gear Solid V: The Phantom Pain is released, Hideo Kojima's final game with Konami.
Rocket League launches on PS4 and PC, becoming an unexpected esports hit.
Super Mario Maker launches for Wii U, letting players create and share their own Mario levels.
Fallout 4 is released by Bethesda, earning $750 million in its first 24 hours.
Hideo Kojima officially departs Konami and establishes Kojima Productions as an independent studio.
Pokémon Go is released by Niantic, becoming a global cultural phenomenon with 500 million downloads in two months.
Overwatch is released by Blizzard Entertainment, popularizing the hero shooter genre.
No Man's Sky launches to controversy over missing promised features.
The Oculus Rift consumer version launches, beginning the modern VR era.
HTC Vive launches with room-scale VR tracking.
PlayStation VR launches as the first major console VR headset.
DOOM (2016) is released by id Software, revitalizing the classic franchise.
Uncharted 4: A Thief's End launches, closing Nathan Drake's story.
Stardew Valley is released by solo developer Eric 'ConcernedApe' Barone.
Final Fantasy XV is released after 10 years in development (originally Final Fantasy Versus XIII).
The Last Guardian finally releases for PS4 after years in development limbo.
Nintendo Switch launches worldwide with The Legend of Zelda: Breath of the Wild.
The Legend of Zelda: Breath of the Wild is released, reinventing the open-world genre.
PlayerUnknown's Battlegrounds (PUBG) enters Early Access on Steam, popularizing the battle royale genre.
Super Mario Odyssey is released for Nintendo Switch.
Fortnite Battle Royale launches as a free-to-play mode, eventually becoming a cultural phenomenon.
Cuphead is released by Studio MDHR, featuring hand-drawn 1930s cartoon-style animation.
Horizon Zero Dawn is released for PS4 by Guerrilla Games.
NieR: Automata is released, directed by Yoko Taro, gaining a massive cult following.
Persona 5 is released in Japan by Atlus.
Hollow Knight is released by Team Cherry, becoming a beloved indie Metroidvania.
The Nintendo Classic Mini: NES sells out instantly, creating massive demand for retro gaming.
Ninja becomes the most-followed Twitch streamer by playing Fortnite, reaching mainstream celebrity status.
God of War (2018) is released for PS4, reinventing the franchise with Norse mythology.
Red Dead Redemption 2 earns $725 million in its first weekend.
Fortnite hosts its first in-game live event, 'Blast Off,' watched by millions of players simultaneously.
Super Smash Bros. Ultimate launches for Nintendo Switch with 74 playable characters.
Celeste is released by Maddy Thorson, acclaimed for both its gameplay and its handling of mental health themes.
Marvel's Spider-Man is released for PS4 by Insomniac Games.
Monster Hunter: World is released, bringing the Monster Hunter franchise to mainstream Western audiences.
Dragon Ball FighterZ launches, developed by Arc System Works, revitalizing anime fighting games.
Epic Games Store launches, challenging Steam's dominance with exclusive titles and a better developer revenue split.
The Dota 2 International 2018 prize pool reaches $25.5 million, a record for esports at the time.
Apex Legends launches by surprise with no prior marketing, gaining 25 million players in its first week.
Sekiro: Shadows Die Twice is released by FromSoftware, winning Game of the Year.
Google Stadia launches, attempting to bring cloud gaming to the mainstream.
Death Stranding is released, Hideo Kojima's first game after leaving Konami.
Star Wars Jedi: Fallen Order revives single-player Star Wars games.
The classic game Tetris is 'beaten' for the first time by a human when 13-year-old Willis Gibson reaches the kill screen.
Disco Elysium is released, redefining the CRPG genre with its narrative-driven approach.
Fire Emblem: Three Houses launches for Nintendo Switch, becoming the best-selling entry in the franchise.
Untitled Goose Game is released by House House, becoming an unexpected indie hit and meme.
Travis Scott performs a virtual concert in Fortnite, attracting 12.3 million concurrent players.
Sony launches the PlayStation 5 in North America.
Microsoft launches the Xbox Series X and Series S.
Animal Crossing: New Horizons is released during the COVID-19 pandemic, selling 13.4 million copies in six weeks.
The Last of Us Part II is released, sparking intense debate over its narrative choices.
Cyberpunk 2077 launches in a notoriously buggy state, leading to its removal from the PlayStation Store.
Fall Guys: Ultimate Knockout launches, becoming a viral multiplayer hit.
Among Us, originally released in 2018, explodes in popularity during the pandemic.
Ghost of Tsushima is released by Sucker Punch Productions.
Hades by Supergiant Games wins multiple Game of the Year awards, becoming a roguelike landmark.
Final Fantasy VII Remake is released for PS4.
Half-Life: Alyx is released by Valve as a VR-exclusive title, the first new Half-Life game in 13 years.
Microsoft acquires ZeniMax Media (Bethesda) for $7.5 billion.
Returnal is released for PS5, showcasing the DualSense controller's haptic feedback.
Metroid Dread is released for Nintendo Switch, the first new 2D Metroid in 19 years.
Resident Evil Village is released by Capcom, with Lady Dimitrescu becoming an internet sensation.
It Takes Two wins Game of the Year at The Game Awards 2021.
Deathloop is released by Arkane Studios, winning multiple awards for its innovative time-loop design.
Valve announces the Steam Deck handheld PC.
Ratchet & Clank: Rift Apart demonstrates the PS5's SSD with near-instant dimension-hopping.
Elden Ring is released by FromSoftware in collaboration with George R.R. Martin, selling 12 million copies in 18 days.
Steam Deck begins shipping to customers.
God of War Ragnarök is released for PS4 and PS5.
Stray is released, letting players explore a cyberpunk city as a cat.
Xenoblade Chronicles 3 launches for Nintendo Switch.
Microsoft announces its intent to acquire Activision Blizzard for $68.7 billion, the largest gaming acquisition ever.
Google announces the shutdown of Stadia, its cloud gaming service.
Splatoon 3 sells 3.45 million copies in Japan in its first three days, a record for any Switch game in the region.
The Legend of Zelda: Tears of the Kingdom is released for Nintendo Switch.
Baldur's Gate 3 officially launches after three years in Early Access, winning Game of the Year.
Starfield is released by Bethesda, the studio's first new IP in 25 years.
Hogwarts Legacy becomes the best-selling game of 2023 despite boycott calls.
Microsoft completes its acquisition of Activision Blizzard for $68.7 billion.
Final Fantasy XVI is released as a PS5 exclusive, directed by Hiroshi Takai.
Alan Wake 2 is released by Remedy Entertainment, 13 years after the original.
Resident Evil 4 Remake is released by Capcom.
The Game Awards 2023 ceremony is interrupted by a stage invader during Baldur's Gate 3's GOTY acceptance.
Diablo IV launches by Blizzard after 11 years since Diablo III.
Apple Vision Pro launches, opening possibilities for spatial gaming.
Palworld launches in Early Access, dubbed 'Pokémon with guns,' selling 25 million copies rapidly.
Helldivers 2 launches, becoming an unexpected co-op hit and the biggest PS5 launch on Steam.
Elden Ring: Shadow of the Erdtree DLC launches, receiving universal acclaim.
Black Myth: Wukong is released by Game Science, the first AAA game from a Chinese studio to achieve global success.
Astro Bot is released for PS5 by Team Asobi, winning Game of the Year at The Game Awards 2024.
Nintendo announces the Switch 2 console.
Space Invaders is released in North America, grossing $2 billion in quarters by 1982.
Frogger is released in arcades by Konami.
Galaga is released in arcades by Namco in Japan.
Nintendo releases Donkey Kong Jr., one of the few games where Mario is the villain.
Activision is founded by former Atari programmers, becoming the first third-party game developer.
The arcade game Asteroids is released by Atari.
Zork I is released by Infocom, defining the text adventure genre.
Ultima I is released by Richard Garriott (Lord British), pioneering the computer RPG.
Nintendo releases Game & Watch handheld series in Japan, designed by Gunpei Yokoi.
Ms. Pac-Man is released in North America by Midway.
The Commodore 64 launches, becoming the best-selling single personal computer model of all time.
Q*bert is released in arcades by Gottlieb.
Dragon's Lair is released in arcades, featuring LaserDisc animation by Don Bluth.
Bomberman debuts on home computers in Japan by Hudson Soft.
Punch-Out!! debuts in arcades in Japan by Nintendo.
King's Quest: Quest for the Crown is released by Sierra On-Line, pioneering graphic adventure games.
Gauntlet is released in arcades by Atari Games, pioneering four-player cooperative gameplay.
Ghosts 'n Goblins is released in arcades by Capcom in Japan.
Bubble Bobble is released in arcades by Taito in Japan.
OutRun is released in arcades by Sega, designed by Yu Suzuki.
Phantasy Star is released for the Sega Master System in Japan, one of the first console RPGs with a female protagonist.
Maniac Mansion is released by Lucasfilm Games, using the revolutionary SCUMM engine.
Contra is released for the NES in Japan.
Double Dragon is released for the NES, defining the beat-'em-up genre.
Mega Man 2 is released in Japan, becoming the best-selling game in the franchise.
John Madden Football is released by Electronic Arts, starting the longest-running sports franchise.
Sega releases Altered Beast as the original pack-in game for the Genesis in North America.
Prince of Persia is released by Jordan Mechner for the Apple II, featuring groundbreaking rotoscoped animation.
The Secret of Monkey Island is released by Lucasfilm Games, designed by Ron Gilbert.
Wing Commander is released by Origin Systems, pushing PC gaming hardware to its limits.
Fire Emblem: Shadow Dragon and the Blade of Light is released in Japan for the Famicom, originating the tactical RPG franchise.
Final Fantasy IV (originally II in NA) introduces the Active Time Battle system.
Sonic the Hedgehog 2 introduces Tails as Sonic's sidekick.
Kirby's Dream Land is released for Game Boy in Japan.
Alone in the Dark is released, pioneering 3D survival horror.
Star Fox is released for the SNES, using the Super FX chip for 3D polygon graphics.
Secret of Mana is released in Japan for the Super Famicom.
Virtua Fighter is released in arcades by Sega AM2, the first 3D polygon fighting game.
NBA Jam is released in arcades by Midway, popularizing over-the-top sports gaming.
Super Metroid is released in Japan for the Super Famicom, defining the Metroidvania genre.
Final Fantasy VI (originally III in NA) is released in Japan, often regarded as the best SNES RPG.
Donkey Kong Country is released for the SNES, featuring pre-rendered 3D graphics by Rare.
Warcraft: Orcs & Humans is released by Blizzard Entertainment, launching the Warcraft universe.
System Shock is released by Looking Glass Technologies, influencing immersive sims for decades.
Earthbound (Mother 2) is released in Japan for the Super Famicom.
The first PlayStation demo disc ships with Official PlayStation Magazine, popularizing demo distribution.
Command & Conquer is released by Westwood Studios, defining the real-time strategy genre.
Tekken 2 is released in arcades by Namco.
Marathon by Bungie is released for Macintosh, a precursor to Halo.
Nights into Dreams is released for Sega Saturn in Japan, designed by Yuji Naka.
Crash Bandicoot is released for PlayStation, developed by Naughty Dog.
Super Mario RPG: Legend of the Seven Stars is released, a collaboration between Nintendo and Square.
Duke Nukem 3D is released by 3D Realms, pushing FPS level interactivity.
Civilization II is released by MicroProse, expanding the acclaimed strategy series.
Parappa the Rapper is released in Japan for PlayStation, popularizing rhythm games.
The first QuakeCon is held in Mesquite, Texas.
Final Fantasy Tactics is released in Japan for PlayStation.
Castlevania: Symphony of the Night is released in Japan for PlayStation, defining Metroidvania.
Age of Empires is released by Ensemble Studios, defining the historical RTS genre.
Fallout is released by Interplay, pioneering the post-apocalyptic RPG.
Odd World: Abe's Oddysee launches for PlayStation.
The Neo Geo Pocket is released by SNK in Japan.
Resident Evil 2 is released for PlayStation in Japan, selling 4.96 million copies.
Xenogears is released in Japan for PlayStation by Square.
Banjo-Kazooie is released for Nintendo 64 by Rare.
Baldur's Gate is released by BioWare, reviving the CRPG genre.
Pokémon Red and Blue are released in North America for Game Boy.
Thief: The Dark Project is released by Looking Glass Studios, pioneering stealth gameplay.
Valve releases Team Fortress Classic as a Half-Life mod.
Silent Hill is released for PlayStation in Japan by Konami.
System Shock 2 is released by Irrational Games and Looking Glass Studios.
Shenmue is released for Dreamcast in Japan, featuring an open world with real-time weather and NPC schedules.
Tony Hawk's Pro Skater is released, defining skateboarding games.
Age of Empires II: The Age of Kings is released, becoming one of the most-played RTS games ever.
Planescape: Torment is released by Black Isle Studios, celebrated as one of the best-written RPGs ever.
Jet Set Radio is released for Dreamcast in Japan, pioneering cel-shaded graphics.
Deus Ex is released by Ion Storm Austin, blending FPS and RPG elements.
Counter-Strike 1.0 is officially released by Valve.
Majora's Mask is released for Nintendo 64 in Japan.
Final Fantasy IX is released in Japan for PlayStation.
Paper Mario is released for Nintendo 64 in Japan.
Hitman: Codename 47 is released by IO Interactive.
Phantasy Star Online launches for Dreamcast in Japan, pioneering console online RPGs.
Pikmin is released in Japan for GameCube, created by Shigeru Miyamoto.
Devil May Cry is released for PS2 in Japan, created by Hideki Kamiya at Capcom.
Ico is released in Japan for PS2 by Team Ico.
Max Payne is released, popularizing bullet time in video games.
Advance Wars is released for Game Boy Advance in North America.
Rez is released for Dreamcast in Japan by Tetsuya Mizuguchi.
RuneScape is released by Jagex, becoming one of the most popular free-to-play MMORPGs.
Animal Crossing is released for GameCube in Japan.
Jak and Daxter: The Precursor Legacy launches for PS2.
Ratchet & Clank is released for PS2 by Insomniac Games.
Splinter Cell is released by Ubisoft, launching the stealth franchise.
Sly Cooper and the Thievius Raccoonus is released for PS2.
Ikaruga is released for GameCube in Japan by Treasure, a revered shoot-'em-up.
Suikoden III is released for PS2 in Japan.
MapleStory launches in South Korea, pioneering the free-to-play MMORPG model.
Beyond Good & Evil is released by Ubisoft, later becoming a cult classic.
Prince of Persia: The Sands of Time revives the franchise with innovative time-manipulation mechanics.
Knights of the Old Republic is released by BioWare for Xbox, widely regarded as the best Star Wars game.
Viewtiful Joe is released for GameCube in Japan by Capcom.
Cave Story is released as freeware by Daisuke 'Pixel' Amaya after five years of solo development.
Katamari Damacy is released in Japan for PS2 by Namco, designed by Keita Takahashi.
Fable is released by Lionhead Studios for Xbox.
Monster Hunter launches in Japan for PS2, beginning the phenomenon that would dominate Japanese gaming.
Doom 3 is released by id Software.
Metal Gear Solid 3: Snake Eater is released in North America.
World of Warcraft reaches 1 million subscribers within its first two months.
God of War is released for PS2, establishing Kratos as a gaming icon.
Nintendogs is released for Nintendo DS in Japan, becoming a system-seller.
Psychonauts is released by Double Fine Productions, directed by Tim Schafer.
Civilization IV is released by Firaxis Games.
The first Ludum Dare game jam takes place.
Defense of the Ancients (DotA) Allstars becomes the most popular Warcraft III custom map, laying the foundation for the MOBA genre.
Dwarf Fortress begins development by Tarn and Zach Adams.
EVE Online launches, creating one of gaming's most complex player-driven economies.
Second Life launches by Linden Lab, becoming a cultural touchstone for virtual worlds.
Okami is released for PS2 in Japan by Clover Studio (Capcom), acclaimed as one of the most beautiful games ever made.
Dead Rising is released for Xbox 360 by Capcom.
Bully is released by Rockstar Games for PS2.
Wii Sports bowling becomes a phenomenon in nursing homes and retirement communities across America.
Crysis is released by Crytek, becoming the benchmark for PC graphics ('Can it run Crysis?').
The World Cyber Games 2001 in Seoul marks the rise of South Korea as an esports powerhouse.
MLG (Major League Gaming) is founded, professionalizing competitive gaming in North America.
Twitch Plays Pokémon begins, with thousands of viewers simultaneously controlling a Game Boy game.
Speedrunner 'Cosmo' Wright sets a famous Ocarina of Time any% world record of 18:10.
Games Done Quick raises over $1 million for charity for the first time at AGDQ 2014.
The first EVO Championship Series (Evolution Championship Series) fighting game tournament is held.
Daigo Umehara performs the legendary 'Evo Moment #37' full parry against Justin Wong in Street Fighter III: 3rd Strike.
League of Legends Season 3 World Championship draws 32 million viewers, held at Staples Center.
The International 2019 Dota 2 prize pool reaches $34.3 million, the largest in esports history at the time.
Faker (Lee Sang-hyeok) wins his third League of Legends World Championship.
The original Doom source code is released by id Software under GPL, enabling the modding community to thrive.
Counter-Strike: Global Offensive (CS:GO) is released by Valve.
Valve releases Team Fortress 2 as free-to-play, pioneering the hat-based cosmetic economy.
The Binding of Isaac is released by Edmund McMillen, becoming a defining roguelike.
Terraria is released by Re-Logic.
Bastion is released by Supergiant Games, their debut title.
Dead Cells is released by Motion Twin, blending roguelike and Metroidvania gameplay.
Slay the Spire is released in Early Access, defining the deckbuilding roguelike genre.
Into the Breach is released by Subset Games.
Outer Wilds is released by Mobius Digital, winning multiple awards for its exploration-based design.
Return of the Obra Dinn is released by Lucas Pope, creator of Papers, Please.
Papers, Please is released by Lucas Pope.
Shovel Knight is released by Yacht Club Games via Kickstarter funding.
The original Game Jam movement gains momentum, inspired by the Indie Game Jam organized by Chris Hecker and others.
Global Game Jam holds its first event across multiple countries simultaneously.
Garry's Mod is released on Steam, pioneering sandbox modding.
The mod Team Fortress for Quake is released, one of the first class-based multiplayer mods.
ARMA 2: DayZ mod reaches 1 million unique players.
The GTA Online heist update launches after years of anticipation.
Fortnite Chapter 2 begins with a black hole event that took the game offline for two days.
The Fortnite Marshmello concert attracts 10.7 million concurrent players.
Pokémon Sword and Shield are released for Nintendo Switch, the first mainline Pokémon games on a home console.
Pokémon Gold and Silver are released in Japan for Game Boy Color.
Pokémon Ruby and Sapphire are released in Japan for Game Boy Advance.
Pokémon Diamond and Pearl are released in Japan for Nintendo DS.
The first Pokémon anime episode airs in Japan.
The 'Pokémon Shock' incident occurs when an episode of the anime causes seizures in nearly 700 Japanese children.
Leeroy Jenkins video is uploaded, becoming one of gaming's most iconic memes.
The Corrupted Blood incident in World of Warcraft creates an unintended virtual pandemic, later studied by epidemiologists.
World of Warcraft's 'Wrath of the Lich King' expansion launches, with players reaching level 80 within 27 hours.
Final Fantasy XI launches in Japan, Square's first MMORPG.
Final Fantasy XIV 1.0 launches to disastrous reviews, leading Square Enix to rebuild it entirely.
Final Fantasy XIV: A Realm Reborn launches, turning the failed MMO into one of the most popular online games.
Square and Enix merge to form Square Enix.
Squaresoft (Square) is founded in Japan.
Capcom is founded in Osaka, Japan.
Konami is founded in Osaka, Japan.
Sega is incorporated in Tokyo, Japan (originally Service Games).
Nintendo is founded by Fusajiro Yamauchi in Kyoto, Japan, as a playing card company.
Sony Computer Entertainment is established, tasked with developing the PlayStation.
Naughty Dog is founded as JAM Software by Andy Gavin and Jason Rubin.
BioWare is founded in Edmonton, Alberta, Canada.
Valve Corporation is founded by former Microsoft employees Gabe Newell and Mike Harrington.
Bungie is founded by Alex Seropian.
Blizzard Entertainment is founded as Silicon & Synapse by Michael Morhaime, Frank Pearce, and Allen Adham.
Rare (originally Ashby Computers and Graphics) is founded by Tim and Chris Stamper in England.
Rockstar Games is founded as a subsidiary of Take-Two Interactive.
FromSoftware is founded in Tokyo, Japan.
CD Projekt is founded in Warsaw, Poland.
Riot Games is founded by Brandon Beck and Marc Merrill.
Epic Games is founded as Potomac Computer Systems by Tim Sweeney.
Satoru Iwata becomes president of Nintendo.
Satoru Iwata, president of Nintendo, passes away at age 55.
Gunpei Yokoi, creator of the Game Boy and designer of the D-pad, dies in a car accident.
Hiromu Takemura and the team at PlatinumGames, founded by former Capcom talent including Hideki Kamiya and Shinji Mikami.
PlatinumGames is officially established in Osaka by former Clover Studio staff.
The first E3 (Electronic Entertainment Expo) is held in Los Angeles.
E3 is cancelled permanently by the ESA after years of declining relevance.
The Tokyo Game Show (TGS) holds its first event.
The Game Awards are founded by Geoff Keighley, held at the Microsoft Theater in LA.
Wii Fit launches in Japan, making the Balance Board a mainstream fitness accessory.
Farming Simulator develops a competitive esports scene in Europe.
The speedrunning community begins organizing around Speed Demos Archive (SDA).
Awesome Games Done Quick (AGDQ) charity speedrunning marathon holds its first event.
Dream's Minecraft speedrun controversy erupts over statistically improbable RNG luck.
Minecraft reaches 238 million copies sold, making it the best-selling video game of all time.
Roblox goes public via direct listing, valued at $41.9 billion.
Epic Games files a lawsuit against Apple after Fortnite is removed from the App Store over payment disputes.
Valve releases the Source 2 engine with Dota 2 Reborn.
Unreal Engine 5 is revealed with the 'Lumen in the Land of Nanite' tech demo.
id Software releases the Doom engine source code, enabling countless total conversion mods.
The first Game Boy Advance emulator appears, sparking debates about game preservation and piracy.
The Nintendo 64DD launches in Japan, becoming one of Nintendo's biggest commercial failures.
The Virtual Boy launches in Japan, Nintendo's first 3D console and one of its biggest failures.
The Atari Jaguar launches, marketed as the first 64-bit console.
The 3DO Interactive Multiplayer launches at $699, pricing itself out of the market.
The Neo Geo AES launches in North America at $649 with arcade-quality games.
The TurboGrafx-16 (PC Engine) launches in Japan.
The Atari Lynx launches, the first handheld with a color LCD screen.
The Sega Game Gear launches in Japan as a portable rival to the Game Boy.
The Nokia N-Gage launches, attempting to combine mobile phones with handheld gaming.
iPhone launches with basic games, eventually transforming mobile gaming.
The App Store launches on iPhone with a games category, enabling the mobile gaming explosion.
Candy Crush Saga launches on Facebook, later becoming one of the most downloaded mobile games.
Clash of Clans is released by Supercell.
Genshin Impact launches globally by miHoYo, grossing $1 billion in mobile revenue within six months.
Wordle goes viral, later acquired by The New York Times.
The New York Times acquires Wordle from creator Josh Wardle.
Valve announces and cancels the Steam Machine initiative.
The first GPD Win handheld PC is shipped, beginning the handheld PC gaming trend.
NVIDIA launches the GeForce 256, marketed as the world's first GPU.
3dfx releases the Voodoo Graphics chip, revolutionizing 3D gaming on PC.
John Carmack implements binary space partitioning (BSP) in Doom, a breakthrough in real-time 3D rendering.
Ray tracing debuts in real-time gaming with NVIDIA's RTX 2080 and Battlefield V.
DLSS (Deep Learning Super Sampling) is introduced by NVIDIA, using AI for real-time upscaling.
Xbox Game Pass launches, pioneering the game subscription model.
PlayStation Now launches as Sony's game streaming service.
OnLive launches as one of the first cloud gaming services, ahead of its time.
Sega Channel launches in the US, an early cable-based game distribution service.
The SegaNet online service launches for Dreamcast in North America.
Xbox Live Arcade launches on Xbox 360, helping popularize downloadable indie games.
Geometry Wars: Retro Evolved becomes a surprise hit on Xbox Live Arcade.
Limbo is released on Xbox Live Arcade, acclaimed for its artistic design.
Fez is released by Polytron Corporation after five years of development.
Spelunky is released on Xbox Live Arcade, defining modern roguelikes.
The indie documentary 'Indie Game: The Movie' premieres at Sundance Film Festival.
Kickstarter becomes a major funding source for games, with Double Fine Adventure raising $3.3 million.
Star Citizen crowdfunding campaign begins, eventually raising over $600 million.
No Man's Sky receives its massive 'Next' update, finally delivering on many original promises.
Cyberpunk 2077 receives its 2.0 update and Phantom Liberty expansion, redeeming the game's reputation.
Valve releases Counter-Strike 2, replacing CS:GO.
Lethal Company becomes a viral hit, developed by solo developer Zeekerss.
Vampire Survivors is released, popularizing the 'bullet heaven' genre.
The Stanley Parable is released, deconstructing player choice in games.
Gone Home is released by Fullbright, advancing the 'walking simulator' genre.
Her Story is released by Sam Barlow, innovating interactive storytelling through FMV.
Disco Elysium's studio ZA/UM undergoes a controversial restructuring, with key creators departing.
Telltale Games shuts down abruptly, laying off most of its staff.
THQ files for bankruptcy.
Acclaim Entertainment files for bankruptcy.
Midway Games files for bankruptcy.
LucasArts is shut down by Disney after acquiring Lucasfilm.
Visceral Games (creators of Dead Space) is shut down by Electronic Arts.
Lionhead Studios (creators of Fable) is closed by Microsoft.
Silicon Knights loses its lawsuit against Epic Games over Unreal Engine issues.
38 Studios, founded by baseball player Curt Schilling, goes bankrupt after Kingdoms of Amalur underperforms.
Irrational Games (BioShock) closes, with Ken Levine starting a smaller studio.
Jack Thompson, anti-video game lawyer, is permanently disbarred in Florida.
The US Supreme Court rules in Brown v. Entertainment Merchants Association that video games are protected speech under the First Amendment.
Loot boxes come under scrutiny as Belgium declares them a form of gambling.
Star Wars Battlefront II's loot box controversy leads EA to temporarily remove microtransactions.
EA's Reddit comment defending Battlefront II microtransactions becomes the most downvoted comment in Reddit history with over 667,000 downvotes.
The Wii U is discontinued by Nintendo, having sold only 13.56 million units.
Nintendo Switch surpasses 100 million units sold.
PlayStation 2 production ends after 12 years, with over 155 million units sold.
The Game Boy line is officially discontinued by Nintendo.
Sega announces the Dreamcast will be its final console.
The Ouya Android-based console launches via Kickstarter, later considered a failure.
Atari files for bankruptcy (the US subsidiary, Atari Inc./Infogrames).
The excavation of Atari's E.T. cartridge burial site in Alamogordo, New Mexico confirms the urban legend.
Nintendo's 'Nintendo Direct' presentation format debuts, bypassing traditional press conferences.
Reggie Fils-Aimé debuts at E3 2004 with 'My name is Reggie, I'm about kickin' ass,' becoming a beloved industry figure.
The PlayStation Network suffers a massive data breach, going offline for 23 days and affecting 77 million accounts.
Xbox One is initially announced with always-online DRM requirements, causing massive backlash and a policy reversal.
The 'Red Ring of Death' hardware failure affects millions of Xbox 360 consoles, costing Microsoft $1.15 billion.
Sony announces the PS3 at E3 2006 with the infamous '$599 US dollars' price point and 'Ridge Racer!' moment.
Blizzard's Diablo Immortal announcement at BlizzCon is met with boos and the 'Do you guys not have phones?' moment.
Nintendo Power magazine publishes its first issue.
Nintendo Power publishes its final issue after 24 years.
Shigeru Miyamoto receives the Person of Cultural Merit award from the Japanese government.
The Video Game Hall of Fame is established at The Strong National Museum of Play in Rochester, NY.
The Smithsonian American Art Museum opens 'The Art of Video Games' exhibition.
The Super Mario Bros. Movie (animated, Illumination) earns over $1.3 billion at the global box office.
Detective Pikachu becomes the first live-action Pokémon film.
The Sonic the Hedgehog movie is delayed to redesign Sonic after fan backlash, later releasing to positive reception.
Arcane, based on League of Legends, premieres on Netflix to critical acclaim.
The Last of Us HBO series premieres, becoming one of the most acclaimed video game adaptations.
The Witcher Netflix series premieres, based on the books that inspired the games.
Cuphead: The Delicious Last Course DLC launches after multiple delays.
Sifu is released by Sloclap, featuring an innovative aging mechanic.
Tunic is released, an indie Zelda-like with a mysterious in-game manual.
Hi-Fi Rush is shadow-dropped during an Xbox Developer Direct, receiving immediate critical acclaim.
Tango Gameworks (creators of Hi-Fi Rush) is shut down by Microsoft, sparking industry outrage.
Embracer Group begins massive layoffs and studio closures after a $2 billion deal falls through.
Over 10,000 game industry workers are laid off in 2023 alone.
Unity announces a controversial per-install runtime fee, causing developer backlash and partial reversal.
Baldur's Gate 3 wins Game of the Year, Best RPG, Best Narrative, Community Vote, and Players' Voice at The Game Awards.
The original Legend of Zelda cartridge sells for $870,000 at auction, a record for a video game.
A sealed copy of Super Mario Bros. sells for $2 million at auction.
The first speedrun of Super Mario Bros. under 5 minutes is achieved.
Nolan Bushnell founds Chuck E. Cheese, using arcade games as the core attraction.
The golden age of arcade games begins, marked by Space Invaders and Pac-Man's cultural dominance.
Billy Mitchell achieves a 'perfect' Pac-Man score of 3,333,360 points.
Billy Mitchell's Donkey Kong high scores are stripped by Twin Galaxies after evidence of using emulation.
The documentary 'The King of Kong: A Fistful of Quarters' brings competitive classic gaming to mainstream attention.
Spacewar!, one of the earliest video games, is created at MIT by Steve Russell and others.
Tennis for Two, often cited as one of the first video games, is created by William Higinbotham at Brookhaven National Laboratory.
The Brown Box prototype by Ralph Baer is demonstrated to television manufacturers.
Computer Space, the first commercially sold coin-operated video game, is released by Nutting Associates.
The Magnavox Odyssey is demonstrated to the public for the first time.
Adventure for the Atari 2600 contains the first known Easter egg in a video game, hidden by programmer Warren Robinett.
Pitfall! is released for the Atari 2600 by Activision, designed by David Crane.
River Raid is released for the Atari 2600, designed by Carol Shaw, one of the first female game designers.
Elite is released for the BBC Micro by David Braben and Ian Bell, pioneering open-world space trading.
Rogue is developed, giving its name to the entire 'roguelike' genre.
MUD1 (Multi-User Dungeon), the first MUD, is created at the University of Essex.
Habitat by Lucasfilm Games launches on QuantumLink (later AOL), one of the first graphical virtual worlds.
Lemmings is released by DMA Design (later Rockstar North).
Civilization is released by MicroProse, designed by Sid Meier.
Sonic & Knuckles introduces 'lock-on technology' allowing combination with previous Sonic cartridges.
Wipeout is released for PlayStation, helping to make the console 'cool' with its electronic music soundtrack.
Virtua Fighter 2 is released in arcades by Sega AM2, pushing 3D fighting game graphics.
Suikoden is released for PlayStation in Japan by Konami.
Harvest Moon (Bokujō Monogatari) is released in Japan for the SNES.
Nights into Dreams launches with the Sega Saturn analog controller, pioneering analog control on consoles.
Mario Kart 64 is released in Japan.
The original Diablo modding scene thrives, leading to total conversion mods.
Rare releases GoldenEye 007's multiplayer mode, which was added late in development almost as an afterthought.
PaRappa the Rapper launches in North America.
Beatmania is released in Japan by Konami, kickstarting the music game genre in Asia.
Dance Dance Revolution is released in arcades in Japan by Konami.
Soulcalibur is released for Dreamcast, the first game to receive a perfect 10 from IGN.
Crazy Taxi is released for Dreamcast in Japan by Sega.
Power Stone is released for Dreamcast, pioneering 3D arena fighting.
Ape Escape is released for PlayStation in Japan, the first game to require the DualShock controller's analog sticks.
Homeworld is released by Relic Entertainment, revolutionizing 3D real-time strategy.
Freelancer is released by Digital Anvil after years of development, originally directed by Chris Roberts.
Neverwinter Nights is released by BioWare, with its toolset enabling massive user-created content.
The Elder Scrolls Construction Set ships with Morrowind, empowering one of gaming's most active modding communities.
Half-Life modding produces Counter-Strike, Day of Defeat, and Team Fortress Classic, proving mods can rival commercial games.
Skyrim modding community creates over 60,000 mods on Nexus Mods.
Bethesda's attempt to introduce paid Skyrim mods on Steam is reversed after community backlash.
Dota Auto Chess mod for Dota 2 goes viral, spawning the auto-battler genre.
The Amiibo NFC figure line launches by Nintendo.
Skylanders: Spyro's Adventure launches, creating the toys-to-life genre.
Disney Infinity launches, entering the toys-to-life market.
Disney Infinity is cancelled and Avalanche Software is closed, ending Disney's internal game development.
Wii Music launches and underperforms critically, showing limits of the Wii's casual gaming approach.
Brain training games become a cultural phenomenon, driving DS sales to over 154 million units worldwide.
Yo-kai Watch launches in Japan, briefly rivaling Pokémon in popularity.
Dragon Quest XI is released in Japan.
Monster Hunter: World ships 21 million units, becoming Capcom's best-selling game ever.
Capcom's Resident Evil 2 Remake becomes a critical and commercial success, revitalizing interest in remakes.
Square Enix sells its Western studios (Crystal Dynamics, Eidos-Montréal) to Embracer Group for $300 million.
Sony acquires Bungie for $3.6 billion.
Take-Two Interactive acquires Zynga for $12.7 billion.
Tencent becomes the world's largest gaming company by revenue through investments in Riot Games, Epic Games, and others.
China implements strict gaming restrictions for minors, limiting play to 3 hours per week.
South Korea repeals its 'Shutdown Law' that banned minors from playing games between midnight and 6 AM.
The WHO officially recognizes 'gaming disorder' as a condition in the ICD-11.
Satoshi Tajiri, creator of Pokémon, reveals that the franchise was inspired by his childhood hobby of insect collecting.
John Carmack joins Oculus VR as CTO, bringing his technical expertise to VR development.
John Carmack resigns from Meta (Oculus) as CTO, citing frustration with corporate inefficiency.
Tim Sweeney creates ZZT, his first shareware game, before founding Epic MegaGames.
Epic MegaGames releases Unreal, launching the Unreal Engine.
Unreal Tournament is released, competing directly with Quake III Arena for FPS dominance.
Quake III Arena is released by id Software.
Halo 2's launch event at a New York GameStop draws thousands of fans in line.
Modern Warfare 2 earns $550 million in five days, setting a new entertainment record.
The Walking Dead Season 1 by Telltale wins multiple GOTY awards, proving narrative games could compete with AAA titles.
Naughty Dog releases Crash Bandicoot 3: Warped, selling over 7 million copies.
Spyro the Dragon is released for PlayStation by Insomniac Games.
Crash Bandicoot N. Sane Trilogy remake launches, proving the market for remasters of classic titles.
Dark Souls III is released, the final entry in the trilogy.
Bloodborne is released exclusively for PS4 by FromSoftware.
Nioh is released by Team Ninja for PS4.
Valheim enters Early Access and sells 5 million copies in one month.
Among Us wins multiple awards at The Game Awards 2020 despite being released in 2018.
The Jackbox Party Pack series becomes a staple of streaming entertainment.
VRChat launches, becoming a major social VR platform.
Beat Saber is released for VR, becoming the best-selling VR game.
Meta Quest 2 becomes the first VR headset to sell over 10 million units.
Half-Life: Alyx demonstrates that AAA VR games are viable.
Wii Sports becomes the fourth best-selling game of all time at 82.9 million copies.
Grand Theft Auto V surpasses 200 million copies sold.
